In brief: Today will be a relatively hot and muggy day in Abuja, with temperatures feeling like 31°C due to the high humidity. As we look ahead to the rest of the week, our attention turns to the increasing chance of rain, which could potentially impact travel plans and outdoor activities. In this forecast, we'll break down the details of what you can expect from the weather over the next seven days, including the potential for some much-needed precipitation.

Currently, the temperature in Abuja is sitting at 26°C, but with a feels-like temperature of 31°C due to the 79% humidity. This is pretty typical for this time of year, and it's a good reminder to stay hydrated and take breaks if you're planning on spending time outdoors. The wind is very light, blowing at just 3 km/h, which means it's not going to provide much relief from the heat. If you're planning on driving, you'll be pleased to know that traffic on the Nnamdi Azikiwe Expressway is currently light, with speeds averaging 37 km/h, which is the same as free-flow traffic.

As we look ahead to the rest of the day, not much is expected to change. We'll see plenty of sunshine, with the UV index currently sitting at 0, indicating that the sun's rays are not particularly strong at the moment. However, as the day wears on, the heat will continue to build, and it's likely to feel quite oppressive by the afternoon. If you're planning on visiting the National Assembly Complex or the Nigerian National Mosque, be sure to bring some water and take breaks in the shade.

### Monday, April 13
Tomorrow is looking like it will be a very similar day to today, with plenty of sunshine and high temperatures. We're expecting a high of 35°C, with a low of 26°C overnight. There is a 20% chance of rain, but at this point, it's looking unlikely that we'll see any significant precipitation. The humidity will remain high, so it's still going to feel quite muggy. If you're planning on driving to work, be aware that the roads may be busier than usual, especially on the Airport Road, but at this point, we're not expecting any major disruptions.

As we head into the day, it's a good idea to keep an eye on the conditions, especially if you have outdoor plans. The heat can be intense, and it's easy to get caught out if you're not prepared. Make sure to wear light, loose-fitting clothing, and bring plenty of water to stay hydrated.
